comments.text: A pile of rocks that always fascinated me in Lake Ontario. Caught at the right time, they look pretty amazing! #Photography #EastCoastKin #longexposure

Found mostly around Lake Superior, these glowing rocks are known as Yooperlites. They are actually syanite rocks rich in fluorescent sodalite - and were only discovered recently! More about Yooperlites and how to find them👇
earthlymission.com/yooperlites-... 📷 Tarah Nicole Hoffman

millions of years tumble

#sensoryart #haiku A Petoskey stone is a fossil colonial coral. These corals lived in warm shallow seas that covered Michigan during Devonian time, some 350 million years ago (Michigan.gov)

If you visit the Upper Peninsula of Michigan & go to the shores of Lake Superior at night, be sure to bring a long-wave UV light source so you can hunt for fluorescent Yooper Stones. "Yooper" here is a play on 'U.P.' (Upper Peninsula), but they're rocks that contain a fluorescent mineral, sodalite.

Hunting for Yooperlites on a Lake Superior beach Yooperlite is a rare luminescent soapstone that glows under ultraviolet light, like a black light. #rockintuesday #tidesouttuesday #coloraday #bluetue #eastcoastkin #artyear #photography

Wulfenite - Arizona, USA
Sulphur - Sicily, Italy
Malachite - Russia

What scientific discovery absolutely blew you away? Of course there are many for me, but this one comes to mind (one I discuss in ENDS OF THE EARTH): freshwater lakes, some the size of the Great Lakes, buried under 2 or more miles of ice in Antarctica. With living creatures inside, no less.

Rock hounds advise searching for Petoskey stones near Lake Michigan. Go while it's raining if you want to make them easier to spot, because the water fills in tiny cracks that scatter light, making the coral's hexagonal structure more apparent.

A whole lotta rocks on the shores of Lake Erie These rocks are up to 400 million years old, and are from the carving out of the lake basin by rivers and glaciers! #RockinTuesday #TidesOutTuesday #EastCoastKin #lakescape #waterscape #landscape #photography #photographersofbluesky
#LakeErie #Ohio

Chapel Rock located along the six mile Chapel Loop Trail. Made of weathered sandstone, this landmark stands near the edge of Lake Superior. It's host to a specimen of Michigan's state tree, the eastern white pine (Pinaceae: Pinus strobus). Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore, Michigan, 05/24/2012.

Sandstone stacked like ancient pages, each layer a chapter from 500 million years ago. At sunset, the cliffs of Pictured Rocks practically read themselves aloud. #RockinTuesday #photography #greatlakes #michigan #sunset #landscape #landscapephotography #nature #rocks #beach #summer

Some rock tumbler photos. This is a batch that’s moving from week two to week three. Upper LH: chevron amethyst, upper RH random jaspers and a piece of opelina, lower RH all stones from Lake Michigan, lower LH tree agate.

The sandstone cliffs of Pictured Rocks rise above Lake Superior like a layered canvas topped with green pines. It was a brutally hot #summer day, so I should have opted to kayak instead of hike, but still beautiful. #BlueSkyArtShow #photography #michigan #cliffs #greatlakes #lakesuperior #nature

What none of you guys know is that Port Puddingstone is only as nice as it is thanks to an Omelas-esque situation where a Silicon Valley techbro is entombed beneath the bed of Lake Huron.
I am a tinkerer who makes art from and of nature. Lately I’ve been focused on driftwood and beach glass rings :) tinkerergoods.etsy.com

One more #RockinTuesday post. I found the rock in the second image below on a Lake Superior beach. I have omars, like the ones in the first picture, but the hole in the second rock is deeper and looks like it was made by hand, maybe for a tool? I could be way off on this. Any opinions? Other ideas?

Kayaking is the best way to enjoy this amazing phenomenon on Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore in Michigan. Minerals leaching from the rock create these brightly colored stripes on the cliffs. #nature #photography #michigan #lakesuperior #landscapephotography #geology #geographicwonder

Apparently it's #RockinTuesday and I have too many rock pix. So here's a video of my favourite beach, where you can walk on 200 million year old fossils. #fossils #GreyCounty #rocks #geology #LakeHuron
#RockinTuesday Rocks under water in clear Lake Superior water to bring out their color.

Super-Size That?
A friend thought this might have been shot somewhere on the Oregon coast, that big rock 40' or 50' tall, a mighty surf curling in from the Pacific. But no. It's the Wisconsin coast and the big rock is 4' or 5' tall on a nice November day.

i'm like a little ADHD gremlin. I was just unpacking and checked the pockets of the shorts I wore this weekend and found rocks from the beach on Lake Superior. I'd completely forgotten that I picked them up.

#ArtAdventCalendar I just finished this one last night! "Mackinac Straits with the Bridge" This is where Michigan's Lower Peninsula meets the Upper Peninsula, and where Lake Michigan meets Lake Huron. I think the boulders might be remnants of the Niagara Escarpment. $289 #traditionalart

7. Yooperlites, collected from Lake Superior in Michigan. These little cuties look boring under normal light, but when hit with a UV light, the sodalite within them exhibits intense fluorescence. This is how you find them - by slowly scanning beaches at night with a UV light!
